name: Lloma Trail
name_original: La Muntanya de Cullera — Circular por Senda de la Lloma (PR-CV 336)
description: Hike the moderate 9.1-mile Mountain of Cullera — Circular via Lloma Trail (PR-CV 336) for panoramic views of Cullera and its coast.
region_intro: Hiking around Cullera offers diverse landscapes, blending mountain trails, river routes, and coastal paths. The region is defined by the prominent Mountain of Cullera, reaching 235 meters, which provides elevated trails and panoramic views. The Júcar River mouth and extensive rice fields, particularly within the Albufera Natural Park, also contribute to the varied terrain. These features create a mix of environments for hikers to explore, from elevated viewpoints to marshy landscapes. Best hiking trails around Cullera The most popular hiking route is Lloma Trail, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route offers panoramic views of Cullera and its coastline, extending into Albufera Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the mountain of Cullera by the Pic dels Francesos — circular by the castle of Cullera, a moderate 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This route passes by Cullera Castle and features accessible paths suitable for most fitness levels. Local hikers also love the Júcar River Mouth and Cullera Castle Trail, an 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and riverbanks, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Cullera is defined by mountain trails, river routes, and coastal paths, with views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Júcar River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to more demanding routes. The routes in Cullera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 2600 hikers have used komoot to explore Cullera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cullera?
A: Cullera offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 70 trails to explore. These range from easy strolls to more challenging routes, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cullera?
A: Hiking in Cullera provides a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find mountain trails on the prominent Mountain of Cullera, scenic river routes along the Júcar River, and beautiful coastal paths with Mediterranean views. The region also features extensive rice fields and marshy areas, particularly within the Albufera Natural Park.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Cullera has plenty of options for beginners and those seeking easier walks. Approximately 30 of the trails are rated as easy, offering accessible paths and less demanding terrain. Many routes on the Mountain of Cullera, for instance, have sections suitable for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cullera?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Cullera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818120274/lloma-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lloma Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.1-mile (14.6 km) circular route offering panoramic views of the coastline and extending into Albufera Natural Park.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views in Cullera?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider trails that ascend the Mountain of Cullera. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376095373/ascent-to-the-mountain-of-cullera-by-the-pic-dels-francesos-circular-by-the-castle-of-cullera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the mountain of Cullera by the Pic dels Francesos&lt;/a&gt; route provides stunning vistas. Additionally, the medieval Cullera Castle, located atop the mountain, offers expansive views of the sea, orange groves, rice fields, and the Júcar River mouth.

Q: Can I explore historical sites or landmarks on a hike in Cullera?
A: Yes, several trails incorporate historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376095373/ascent-to-the-mountain-of-cullera-by-the-pic-dels-francesos-circular-by-the-castle-of-cullera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the mountain of Cullera by the Pic dels Francesos&lt;/a&gt; passes directly by Cullera Castle. You can also find other points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita dels Benissants&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2690012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Garrofer&lt;/a&gt; along various routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like rivers or the coast?
A: Cullera is known for its beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376095134/jucar-river-mouth-and-cullera-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Mouth and Cullera Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads through coastal areas and along riverbanks, offering views of the Júcar River's entry into the Mediterranean. Many routes also provide stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea and pass through marshy landscapes within the Albufera Natural Park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Cullera?
A: The hiking trails in Cullera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from mountain and coastal views to river and rice field scenery, as well as the well-maintained paths and the variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cullera?
A: Many of Cullera's trails are suitable for families, especially the easier routes and those with accessible paths. The diverse scenery, including the castle and coastal views, can keep younger hikers engaged. Always check the specific trail's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Cullera?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Cullera, especially in natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash and check for any specific local regulations or protected areas where restrictions might apply. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural beauty of the trails.

Q: Are there any caves or unique natural monuments to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, Cullera offers some unique natural features. While hiking, you might encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2690012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Garrofer&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking more adventure, some experiences in Cullera combine hiking with cave exploration, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3297906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Galera&lt;/a&gt;, which can involve ropes for deeper exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cullera?
A: Cullera's mild Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are particularly pleasant, with comfortable temperatures and lush scenery. Winter offers cooler, crisp days perfect for hiking, while summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.
tips: High on the top of the mountain is the imposing fortress of the Castle of Cullera. Built by the state of Cordoba in the 9th century for the strategic control of natural resources, communication routes and borders, it is made up of the alcazaba and two albacaras that fulfilled the function of sheltering the troops, cavalry, cattle and the population itself at times of siege
Without a doubt, this point offers the best views of the Bay of Cullera and the rice fields of Sueca. One of my favorite routes, to travel the entire rope of the mountain!

A site that offers amazing views of the Levantine coast and the beaches of Cullera. It can be walked on the sidewalk and around a path between the rocks near the cliff. A very picturesque panoramic site!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike The Mountain of Cullera — Circular via Lloma Trail (PR-CV 336)?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this moderate circular hike ranges from approximately 3 hours 16 minutes to 4 hours 25 minutes, depending on your pace and stops. The total duration is around 15,898 seconds, which is about 4 hours and 25 minutes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the PR-CV 336?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied experience with diverse terrain. You'll encounter a mix of paved sections (around 5.68 km), natural paths (around 2.63 km), asphalt, unpaved sections, and dedicated mountain hiking paths. It traverses roads, streets, and footpaths, including ascents along the crest of the Cullera mountain, which are generally manageable.

faqs.question: What kind of views and scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: Hikers can expect spectacular and varied scenery. The trail provides extensive panoramic views of the Júcar River (Xúquer) mouth, the town of Cullera, its coastline, and surrounding agricultural plains. You'll also see vistas towards the sea and the interior, including the Bassa de Sant Llorenç lagoon and nearby mountain ranges. Key viewpoints include the View of Cullera from the Castle and the View of Cullera Bay and Sueca Rice Fields from Meteorological Center.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or historical sites on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several points of interest. You'll pass by the ruins of El Fort (Fort of Liberty), the Cullera Castle and Sanctuary, and archaeological remnants like the "Volcán" site. The trail also leads to the Cullera Lighthouse, a historic circular building, and highlights the ancient sand dunes of Playa del Dosser.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, the trail can be started from various points. A common starting point is near the municipal cemetery, which typically offers parking. Alternatively, you can begin directly from the Lighthouse area.

faqs.question: Is The Mountain of Cullera — Circular via Lloma Trail (PR-CV 336) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially since parts of the trail are within the Albufera Natural Park. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations regarding pets in protected areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat of the Mediterranean coast. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming flora, while autumn provides comfortable hiking conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Albufera Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the PR-CV 336 trail within the Albufera Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated regulations or special permits that might be needed for certain activities or areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Senda de la Lloma - Muntanya de Cullera, El Camí IT5TR6 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Passeig Marítim, el Calvari, and Barri del Pou.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The PR-CV 336 is classified as a moderate hike, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. While older children who are accustomed to hiking might enjoy the varied terrain and views, it may be challenging for very young children or those new to hiking due to its length and elevation gain.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. A map or GPS device is also useful, although the trail is generally well-marked. Binoculars can enhance your experience of the panoramic views.
